critique.summary: The article outlines a limited-time promotion for Claude users that doubles usage limits during off-peak hours from March 13 to March 27, 2026. This offer applies to various plans but excludes Enterprise plans.
mainPoints: Promotion doubles usage limits for Claude users outside 8 AM-2 PM ET/5-11 AM PT.
Applicable to Free, Pro, Max, and Team plans; Enterprise plans are excluded.
No action required from users to receive the doubled usage.
Bonus usage during the promotion does not count against weekly limits.
critique.truthfulness: The claims made in the article appear to be accurate based on the provided details about the promotion. The dates, eligibility, and specifics of the offer are clearly stated and consistent with typical promotional practices.
considerations: The article does not mention potential impacts on user experience during peak hours.
It does not clarify how the promotion might affect new users or those considering upgrading their plans.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the promotional usage of Claude in March 2026, with users speculating on the psychological tactics behind the promotion and its implications for usage limits. Some comments reflect skepticism about the motives, while others seek clarification on the specifics of the promotion.

comments.author: twtw99
comments.text: This is great, but i guess they are feeling the heat from Codex resetting limits in the last month quite a bit.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a relevant observation about market competition, indicating a thoughtful understanding of the context.

comments.author: JoshGlazebrook
comments.text: I just know there has to be some psychology in play with these promos. The promo during December got me to upgrade to the $100 plan...
comments.analysis: This comment insightfully connects personal experience with broader psychological tactics, adding depth to the discussion.

comments.author: colingauvin
comments.text: Presumably they have unused compute in those hours and figure they may as well enable people to use it and get more invested into their ecosystem...
comments.analysis: This comment presents a logical hypothesis about the promotion's purpose, demonstrating an understanding of business strategy.

comments.author: timmg
comments.text: I’m trying to figure out how this affects weekly limits, since those overlap peak hours. My observation is that it doesn’t...
comments.analysis: The comment raises a valid question about the promotion's mechanics, contributing constructively to the discussion.

comments.author: estebarb
comments.text: I didn't understood "your five-hour usage" I thought plans were per interaction or per token, not per hour.
comments.analysis: This comment expresses confusion about the promotion's details, which is relevant but lacks depth in analysis.

comments.author: egeozcan
comments.text: So afternoon in Germany or am I misreading?
comments.analysis: While this comment seeks clarification, it does not contribute significantly to the overall discussion.

comments.author: michaelhoney
comments.text: Living in Tasmania as competitive advantage
comments.analysis: This comment appears to be a lighthearted remark but lacks substance and relevance to the main discussion.

comments.author: andkenneth
comments.text: This is a psyop to recruit more Australians I'm sure of it
comments.analysis: This comment is speculative and conspiratorial, detracting from the constructive nature of the discussion.

comments.author: candeira
comments.analysis: This comment adds little to the conversation and seems more like a casual remark than a substantive contribution.
